name: StaticArray

aliases.name: StaticArray
aliases.comment:
A static array is an array with a fixed length.

```
singleElement : StaticArray One Int
singleElement =
    singleton 42

twoElements : StaticArray Two Int
twoElements =
    singleElement
    |> push 7
```

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Constructs an array from a list
values.type: StaticArray.Length.Length n -> a -> List.List a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a

values.name: fromRecord
values.comment:
Constructs a static array from its components.
If the Length is bigger then the number of elements provided, the additional element will be filled with duplicates of head.

```
fromRecord
    { length = (Length.five |> Length.plus1 )
    , head = 0
    , tail = [1,2,3,4,5]
    }
    --> fromList (Length.five |> Length.plus1 ) [0,1,2,3,4,5]
```
values.type: { length : StaticArray.Length.Length n, head : a, tail : Array.Array a } -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a

values.name: get
values.comment: Gets an element of the array. Note that it only possible if the index is in bound. Therefore eliminating Off-by-one errors.
values.type: StaticArray.Internal.Index n -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> a

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment: Same as map but with an index.
values.type: (StaticArray.Internal.Index n -> a -> a) -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a

values.name: length
values.comment: Returns the length of an array.

values.name: map
values.comment: Updates all elements of an array.
values.type: (a -> a) -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a

values.name: push
values.comment: Adds a element to the end of the array.
values.type: a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> StaticArray.StaticArray (StaticArray.Index.OnePlus n) a

values.name: resize
values.comment:
Changes the size of the array. If the array gets bigger, then the first element of the array gets used as default value.

```
singleton 42
|> push 7
|> resize (Length.one |> Length.plus4)
|> toArray
|> Array.toList --> [42,7,42,42,42]
```
values.type: StaticArray.Length.Length m -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> StaticArray.StaticArray m a

values.name: set
values.comment: Sets an element of the array.
values.type: StaticArray.Internal.Index n -> a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> StaticArray.StaticArray n a

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Constructs an array with a single item.

values.name: toArray
values.comment: Transforms the static array into a dynamic array.

values.name: toRecord
values.comment:
Exposes the underlying record of the array

```
array =
    [0,1,2,3,4,5]
        |> fromList (Length.five |> Length.plus1 )

array |> toRecord |> fromRecord --> array
```
values.type: StaticArray.StaticArray n a -> { length : StaticArray.Length.Length n, head : a, tail : Array.Array a }

name: StaticArray.Index

aliases.name: Index
aliases.comment: An Index is integer between 0 and n-1

values.name: decrease
values.comment: Increases the index by one. You can not decrease the first index.

values.name: first
values.comment: The first index of an array

values.name: fromLessThen
values.comment:
Construct an Index by cutting off higher values

```
fromModBy (Length.one |> Length.plus2) 3
--> first |> increase |> increase --2
```

values.name: fromModBy
values.comment:
Construct an Index by wrapping higher values

```
fromModBy (Length.one |> Length.plus2) 3
--> first --0
```

values.name: increase
values.comment: Increases the index by one. You can not increase the last index.
values.type: StaticArray.Internal.Length n -> StaticArray.Index.Index n -> Maybe.Maybe (StaticArray.Index.Index n)

values.name: last
values.comment: The last element of an array with length n

values.name: range
values.comment: Returns the list of all indexes of an array with length n

values.name: setLength
values.comment: Change the range of the index. If the length is smaller then the index, it returns Nothing.
values.type: StaticArray.Internal.Length m -> StaticArray.Index.Index n -> Maybe.Maybe (StaticArray.Index.Index m)

values.name: toInt
values.comment: Convert to Integer. You can convert it back by using either `fromModBy` or `fromLessThen`

name: StaticArray.Length

aliases.name: Length
aliases.comment:
A length represents the length of a static array

```
StaticArray.singelton 42
    |> StaticArray.push 7
    |> StaticArray.length
    --> two
```

values.comment: Length of a array with eight elements
